verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone, but under stress conditions (cancer, viral infection, ischemia, unfolded protein response), a documented fraction translocates to the cell surface where it acts as a co-receptor and signaling hub. Surface GRP78 has been validated as a therapeutic target by multiple antibody and ADC programs that engage it on the outer leaflet of tumor cells. This qualifies as cell-state-induced surface exposure of the protein body, even though the dominant localization is ER-lumenal.
